description: Double-ice Abomasnow lead with Shadow Twister Gyarados safe swap and Water Gun Quagsire closer. Team stolen from Exeutor on Twitter, designed as an early Expert/Veteran build for the Jungle Cup.

performanceNotes: Abomasnow's Icy Wind debuffs make cleanup easy for Quagsire and Gyarados
Shadow Gyarados with Twister applies strong fast pressure and baiting potential
Fortress is the hardest counter — if you don't face it, the team runs smoothly

description: Triple shadow team with Shadow Abomasnow as a core-breaking lead that shuts down water types with grass/ice coverage. Gyarados covers Abomasnow's fire and fighting weaknesses, while Shadow Quagsire closes with spammy Aqua Tails and Stone Edge nukes.

description: The stronger of the two teams per the creator. Shadow Camerupt lead with Furret as a strong counter-swap to Quagsire safe switches, and Altaria as closer. A trainer used this team to hit 2600 ELO.

Used by a trainer to reach 2600 ELO

description: Water Gun Lanturn lead with Altaria safe swap and Togekiss closer in Jungle Cup. Togekiss provides Aura Sphere and Psyshock coverage which is strong in this format.

Shadow Talonflame lead in an ABB-style team designed as a hard meta read for Jungle Cup. Talonflame forces switches with double-shield pressure, while the back two (Altaria and Water Gun Quagsire) cover each other's weaknesses and rely on alignment. Team submission from Nerf Rampidos.
performanceNotes: Talonflame has two-shield win conditions against most of the top 10 meta picks
Criminally weak to dragons, Water Gun Lanturn, and Gyarados
Creator explicitly notes this is not recommended for climbing as the meta adapts

Umbreon's insane bulk allows it to survive even at low HP, enabling foul play spam

description: Classic core of Swampert + Corviknight with Umbreon as the OG safe swap. Corviknight and Umbreon provide enough bulk to save shields for Swampert's Hydro Cannon spam.

Dunsparce lead with Qwilfish safe swap running the unusual Scald set instead of Ice Beam, and Gourgeist as closer. The team revolves around building energy on Qwilfish and leveraging Scald's cheap cost and high neutral damage to sweep.

Dunsparce added specifically to handle the rise of Stunfisk leads that destroyed the previous Togekiss lead

Achieved a 13-game win streak, reaching close to 2600 ELO

Whimsicott double-resists Gyarados's Aqua Tail + Twister making it the hardest counter in the meta

Waterfall Gyarados lead with double shadow ground backline. Gyarados applies fast move pressure with buffed Waterfall while Shadow Gliscor and Shadow Flygon provide Sand Tomb debuff spam and ground-type coverage in the back.

Double ground backline with Sand Tomb spam forces shields and drops defense

A debuff-heavy team that overwhelms opponents with fast move pressure combined with attack and defense drops from Icy Wind, Psychic Fangs, and Poison Fang. The creator copied this team from an opponent faced in a previous video.
